rows.source_ref: sharpiemomo, "Simple System", ForexFactory forum thread #1184099 (year not stated, n.d.), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1184099-simple-system
rows.concept: Enter on the first opposite-color close of a custom modified-Heiken-Ashi bar (APB) as a momentum-rejection reversal scalp on US30 during the NY open, stopping at the prior swing.
rows.eligibility_reason: Exit and sizing are wholly discretionary (author states "no TP rule", takes partials ~0.5R by instinct, scratches stalling trades by feel, sizes via subjective "clicks"); entry depends on an undisclosed proprietary "APB" (modified Heiken Ashi) indicator plus paid range bars, and setup quality is explicitly experience/instinct-based -- no mechanically complete rule set to implement.
rows.notes: Entry kernel (Heiken-Ashi color-flip reversal, stop at prior swing high/low) is the only mechanizable part but is a classic discretionary chart pattern with no structural edge -- congruent with the closed Wyckoff/SMC/chart-pattern doctrine. Author trades US30 09:30-12:00 EST (also formerly NASDAQ/NDX), M15 and M5 plus paid range bars; risk = "Risk Per Click" ($250/click, max 4, MFR $1k). Community members (demon83, MrEnterprise) tried and failed to extract a mechanical exit; author rejected the 5-EMA-touch TP suggestion. Thread is 47 pages but content after p.4 is trade-log screenshots and Q&A with no additional rules. Uses FTMO demo for practice only.
rows.market_note: US30 = WS30 (DXZ tradable); NDX also available; range-bar data not native to the time-TF tester.

notes: Single REJECTED summary row: no mechanically complete candidate. Discretionary scalping system around undisclosed modified-HA indicator + paid range bars; exit/sizing 100% discretionary.
Thread = 60 posts, 3 pages of the original thread flattened into 47 PDF pages; pages 5-47 are almost entirely 'Attached Image' trade screenshots and chatter with no rule content.
